payload.answer: Saved fetch failure and the user's intent only; did not save any article content claims.
payload.decision: Store only the access/fetch metadata and the user's stated intent; do not store any claims about article contents because the fetch returned an error (404).

entity_resolution.reason: No canonical entity IDs provided; only a URL and fetch status are available.

payload.intent: Record user-requested memory about an article URL for later use in memory architecture, while accounting for fetch failure.

memory_cards.statement: An attempt was made to retrieve the article at https://example.com/missing for memory-architecture purposes, but the mock fetch result was 404 (network error), so the article content was not retrieved.
User stated the (unretrieved) article is useful for memory architecture; the usefulness claim is unverified due to the fetch failure.

details: Recorded URL fetch failure (404 network error).
Recorded that the user described the article as useful for memory architecture, marked as unverified.

repair_options: Provide the article text or a working URL so content can be extracted and stored.
Retry fetching the URL later (in case the 404 was temporary).
Share key excerpts or a summary that can be stored without relying on live fetch.
